text: Graph representation learning supports scientific discovery across many important application domains and increasingly complex relational datasets.
This practical need motivates a robust learning strategy that can operate reliably across diverse deployment environments and datasets.
To address this challenge, we introduce a modular framework that combines complementary evidence and supports reliable adaptation across domains.
The proposed method consists of an encoder, an evidence fusion module, and a calibrated prediction head trained jointly for robust inference.
We adopt a gated fusion mechanism because it allows the model to suppress unreliable evidence while preserving complementary information.
Compared with the strongest baseline, our method achieves higher accuracy across every evaluated dataset and experimental configuration.
A remaining limitation is the restricted evaluation scale, which cannot establish robustness for substantially larger deployment settings.
Future work will investigate broader deployment settings and explore adaptation under more diverse distribution shifts and resource constraints.
